mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses Maya civilization across Classic and Post-Classic periods, including monumental architecture at Tikal and Palenque (B.2.c.i), hieroglyphic writing and calendar systems (B.2.c.i), the mysterious abandonment of major cities during the Terminal Classic (B.2.c.ii), and Post-Classic developments at Chichen Itza (C.1). Also contributes to the broader characterization of Meso-American civilization and its achievements (A).

Covers the rise of the Aztec Triple Alliance empire, its expansion over two centuries to encompass 500 tributary states and six million people (C.2), and Aztec culture including political organization, tribute systems, urban planning at Tenochtitlan, religious cosmology with human sacrifice, and the Spanish conquest under Cortes (C.3). The discussion of enduring cultural legacy in modern Mexican identity also addresses Meso-American civilization's character and achievements broadly (A).

Focuses on the 1521 fall of the Aztec capital, directly addressing the culmination of Montezuma II's reign and the Spanish conquest (C.2), as well as Aztec political organization, militarism, and the alliance dynamics with indigenous peoples such as the Tlaxcalans that ended Aztec rule (C.3). The episode's treatment of the city's island setting in Lake Texcoco and the siege tactics illuminates Aztec urban and military infrastructure.
